eras.name: Magyar Conquest Era
eras.description: The nomadic heritage of the Magyars characterized by leatherwork and functional garments for horse riding. Clothing was heavily influenced by Central Asian and Persian aesthetics.
eras.aiImagePrompt: Historical illustration of a 10th-century Magyar warrior and noblewoman, wearing heavy fur-trimmed kaftans, intricate silver belt buckles, and conical hats, set against the Carpathian basin.

eras.name: Renaissance Splendor
eras.description: Under King Matthias Corvinus, the court in Buda became a center of European fashion, blending Italian Renaissance elegance with Eastern ornate details.
eras.aiImagePrompt: A 15th-century Hungarian nobleman in the court of Matthias Corvinus, wearing a velvet dolman with gold embroidery and fur lining, holding a feathered cap.

eras.name: Baroque & Ottoman Influence
eras.description: A period of 'Hussar' style development, where military uniforms and aristocratic dress featured heavy braiding, frogging, and vivid colors.
eras.aiImagePrompt: A Baroque-era Hungarian hussar in a scarlet jacket with elaborate gold cord frogging and a fur-lined pelisse slung over one shoulder.

eras.name: The Reform Era
eras.description: Fashion became a political statement of national identity against Austrian influence, leading to the 'Díszmagyar' (formal national dress).
eras.aiImagePrompt: A 19th-century Hungarian noblewoman wearing a Díszmagyar gown with white lace, heavy floral embroidery, and a traditional párta crown.

eras.name: Budapest Belle Époque
eras.description: Budapest rivaled Paris in sophistication; Art Nouveau influences merged with traditional folk motifs in high-end tailoring.
eras.aiImagePrompt: Elegant socialites in 1900s Budapest walking along Andrássy Avenue, wearing Art Nouveau inspired lace dresses and wide-brimmed feathered hats.

eras.name: Socialist Goulash Fashion
eras.description: A shift towards industrial uniformity followed by the 'Goulash Communism' era, where Western trends were cautiously adopted through state-run department stores.
eras.aiImagePrompt: 1970s Budapest street scene showing citizens in colorful nylon housecoats and early Eastern Bloc denim, featuring Socialist-era architecture in the background.

eras.name: The Post-Communist New Wave
eras.description: A resurgence of independent design houses in Budapest, focusing on avant-garde silhouettes and the reinterpretation of Matyó and Kalocsa embroidery.
eras.aiImagePrompt: A modern fashion editorial in the ruins of a Budapest ruin bar, featuring a model in a minimalist deconstructed black coat with subtle neon embroidery.

textiles.name: Matyó Embroidery
textiles.description: Characterized by densely packed, vibrant floral patterns, most notably the 'Matyó Rose'.

textiles.name: Kalocsa Lace
textiles.description: Brightly colored floral patterns on white or black fabric, often featuring 'richelieu' cutwork.
textiles.technique: Machine and hand embroidery with openwork

textiles.name: Kékfestő (Blue-dye)
textiles.description: Indigo-dyed fabric with white resist-printed patterns, historically used for peasant clothing.
textiles.technique: Resist block printing followed by indigo vat immersion

textiles.name: Halas Lace
textiles.description: Exquisite, needle-point lace that is often compared to Brussels lace in its intricacy.

climate.fashionImplication: Extreme seasonal variance necessitates a wardrobe of layered wool coats for harsh winters and breathable linens for hot, humid summers.
